steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
Since angle B is the right angle, AC is the hypotenuse. We have:
$$AC^2 = (10\sqrt{39})^2 + (24\sqrt{39})^2$$
Squaring each term:
$$AC^2 = 100 \cdot 39 + 576 \cdot 39 = 39(100 + 576) = 39 \cdot 676$$

steps.title: Simplify the radical
steps.content:
Taking the square root of both sides:
$$AC = \sqrt{39 \cdot 676} = \sqrt{39} \cdot \sqrt{676} = \sqrt{39} \cdot 26 = 26\sqrt{39}$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
